rows.notes: Clear centered year at top of page, no amounts present; very legible.
Clear title line; handwriting of 'Rectoris S.L. Richards' is legible though flourished.
Latin date phrase clearly written below title, no monetary columns used.
Second Latin phrase under dates; legible and clearly a header.
Section header (Arrearages Receipts). Slight ambiguity in spelling ('Arrearagia') due to handwriting flourishes.
Clear sub-header under 'Arrearagia Recepta'.
Amount '60' clearly aligned in the leftmost money column (pounds). Description letters somewhat stylized but readable as 'Gwinear Rectory'.
Single number '10' in pounds column. 'Kidlington' is clearly legible; 'do' (ditto) indicates same as above.
Three-part amount '1 7 6' clearly aligned as £1 7s 6d. Description legible.
Amount '9 6 3' clearly in pounds/shillings/pence columns. 'Land Lane, Norris' read from handwriting; minor uncertainty on exact placename spelling but amount alignment is unambiguous.
Amount '6 13 4' clear. The place name is transcribed as 'Dunnithorne' — handwriting makes vowels slightly uncertain but context indicates a placename; numbers align well.
Amount '1 13 4' clearly present. 'Rickford' transcription is reasonable given the script; amount columns are well aligned.
Amount '6 13 4' clear. Place name 'Medenham' read from script; letters slightly ornate but identifiable; currency columns unambiguous.

Amount shows three items '1 19 1 1/2' interpreted as £1 19s 1½d (pence fraction 0.5). Description uncertain: reading of placename is ambiguous ('Little Tow Bocks' is best phonetic match to the handwriting); lower confidence on description.

Only two numerals '17 6' appear and are aligned under shillings and pence columns (no pounds), interpreted as 17s 6d. Place name 'Lymath' is moderately uncertain due to script; monetary column alignment clear.
Single '7' visible aligned under shillings column (no pounds or pence shown) — interpreted as 7s. 'Lennard' reading of the name is somewhat uncertain from handwriting.
Amount '10' clearly in pounds column. 'Bushey Tenements' is legible in script.
New section header clearly written; no amounts on this header line.
Amount '11 2 9' aligns clearly as £11 2s 9d. Description legible.
Single '28' in pounds column. 'L Wittenham' interpreted as 'Long Wittenham?' but page shows 'L Wittenham' — reading of initial is clear; amount alignment is unambiguous.

Two numbers '4 4' present. They are aligned in pounds and shillings columns (no pence), giving £4 4s. 'Do Vicarage' (ditto) is legible; small uncertainty whether last column had a faint pence which is not visible.
Amount '11' in pounds column clear. 'Yarnton' legible from handwriting.
Section header clearly written, indicating next group of variable rents.
Amount '84 0 6' clearly aligns as £84 0s 6d. Description 'Kidlington Augmentation' is legible.
Amount '34 8 4' clear and in proper columns. 'Do Mills' (ditto Mills) is readable.
Amount '107 14 3' evidently £107 14s 3d. 'Long Wittenham Rectory' identified; script is clear.

rows.description: Sth Newington Rectory 1 yr to 2 Day 1838
rows.notes: Amount '181 19 9' clear as £181 19s 9d. Description reads 'Sth Newington Rectory 1 yr to 2 Day 1838' with minor uncertainty on abbreviation 'Sth' (South).
Amount reads '2 14 8 1/4' aligned as £2 14s 8¼d. Fraction after pence is small but clearly a farthing (¼d) symbol; transcription reflects that.
Amount appears as '239 7 5 1/4' (£239 7s 5¼d). The fraction is a small farthing symbol visible after the pence; description text in parentheses is readable though somewhat crowded.
Amount '2 10 10 1/2' interpreted as £2 10s 10½d (pence fraction 0.5). Place name 'Tintinhull' and 'Beaton' readable though with typical 19th-century flourish.
Amount '15 13 3 3/4' read as £15 13s 3¾d (pence fraction 0.75). Description 'Penny Do Do' (ditto) is plausible from script; fraction is visible as three‑quarters symbol.
Final total line clearly written at page bottom as '£ 834 7 3 1/4' (£834 7s 3¼d). Currency sign and alignment clear; fraction appears as a farthing (¼d).
